articles.title: Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib killed in air strike, Israel says
articles.body: Iran has retaliated with strikes across the Middle East. On Wednesday, two people were killed in a strike on Israel, and blasts and drone interceptions were reported in Dubai, Qatar, Kuwait, Iraq, Bahrain and Saudi Arabia.

articles.title: Israel destroys river bridges in southern Lebanon
articles.body: Bystanders stood in shock, surveying the destruction. Tensions quickly rose among onlookers. Some shouted angrily, directing blame at Israel, while a group of men raised a tall poster of Hezbollah's slain leader, Hassan Nasrallah, marching it to the top of the pile of rubble.

articles.title: Uganda: 40 years after the last one was poached, rhinos are back in the wild
articles.body: The UWA said a secure rhino sanctuary outfitted with perimeter fencing, access roads, firebreaks, ranger facilities, water systems and monitoring technology was in place to ensure the animals are effectively protected and managed.

articles.title: Russian archaeologist can be sent to Ukraine for trial, rules Polish judge

articles.title: Marius Borg Høiby rape trial: Prosecution asks for seven years' jail for Norwegian crown princess's son
articles.body: Prosecutors in the trial of Marius Borg Høiby, son of Norwegian Crown Princess Mette-Marit, have requested a jail term of seven years and seven months, arguing he should be found guilty of 39 of the 40 charges he is accused of.

articles.title: Ozempic, Wegovy: India's cheap weight-loss drugs could reshape global obesity fight
articles.body: Several Indian drugmakers are already preparing to make the move. According to Sheetal Sapale, vice-president at research firm Pharmarack, major firms including Cipla, Sun Pharma, Dr Reddy's Laboratories, Biocon, Natco, Zydus and Mankind Pharma are readying branded generics, with many more likely to follow. Prices are expected to fall sharply.

articles.title: LNG: Will the Iran war squeeze India's piped gas next?
articles.body: "No disruption is expected for homes and vehicles [using piped gas]. The government has given priority to these two sectors," says Rahul Chopra, managing director for the Haryana City Gas Distribution Limited, a countrywide gas company with around 100,000 domestic consumers and 195 CNG fuel stations.

articles.title: Gabbard faces scrutiny over Iran nuclear intelligence claims
articles.body:
The US Director of National Intelligence, Tulsi Gabbard, was questioned over what Democrats perceive as a discrepancy between White House and intelligence community claims on Iran's nuclear enrichment capabilities.

She wrote in her pre-written opening statement to the Senate Intelligence Committee; 'As a result of Operation Midnight Hammer, Iran’s nuclear enrichment program was obliterated. There has been no efforts since then to try to rebuild their enrichment capability.'

But in her oral statement she said, 'prior to Operation Epic Fury, the IC assesses Iran was trying to recover from the severe damage to its nuclear infrastructure sustained during the 12 Day War and continued to refuse to comply with its nuclear obligations with the IAEA, refusing them access to key facilities.'

When pressed by Democratic senators, she said that she changed her statement to save time.

articles.title: Iditarod dog sled race ends in repeat win for Jessie Holmes
articles.body:
Jessie Holmes has won his second consecutive Iditarod Trail Sled Dog Race in Nome, Alaska. The former reality TV star completed the roughly 1,000-mile (1,609-kilometer) course in nine days, seven hours, and 32 minutes.

As this year’s winner, Holmes received an $80,000 prize. He also earned an additional $5,500 in cash and $4,500 in gold nuggets for sweeping every special award along the trail.

Holmes made his Iditarod debut in 2018 and has competed every year since, earning five top-10 finishes in addition to his two victories.

articles.title: Venezuelans celebrate historic WBC victory over USA
articles.body:
Venezuelans around the world are celebrating a historic baseball victory after Venezuela captured its first-ever World Baseball Classic title.

The team defeated the United States 3–2, with Eugenio Suárez breaking the tie in the top of the ninth inning.

The win comes at a time of political turmoil for the country following the US government's seizure of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro during a military raid in January.

articles.title: Why did only two Iranian football players stay in Australia?
articles.body:
After Australia granted seven Iranian football players humanitarian visas to stay in the country, only two have decided to stay.

According to human rights activists, those who left feared reprisals from the Iranian Government, but the semi-official Mehr news agency called it a "patriotic decision".

The footballers had originally sought sanctuary after concerns they would face repercussions for staying silent during the country's anthem at their opening Asian Cup match.
